era: 1960-1975 core. Garage Rock / Bubblegum Rock. E=6 -- organ-driven, costume-wearing, Mark Lindsay's voice. Boise / Portland / Seattle.

key_hits.title: Kicks
key_hits.notes: Anti-drug; Barry Mann/Cynthia Weil; band signature

theory_nodes.concept: TWO-THIRDS MARK: Degree 240/360 = 66.7% completion. The engine has documented 240 S36 artists and 240 SPS species. 120 degrees remain. This is the structural milestone of the third rotation quarter.
theory_nodes.description: 240/360 = two-thirds of the full 360-degree rotation completed.

theory_nodes.concept: Paul Revere and the Raiders: Pacific Northwest garage rock, 1960-1975. Boise origin, Portland/Seattle establishment. Revolutionary War costumes. Columbia Records. Where the Action Is TV house band. First Pacific Northwest band to achieve sustained national commercial visibility.
theory_nodes.description: The Raiders as the first nationally prominent Pacific Northwest rock band.

theory_nodes.concept: Columbia Records thread: PUSA (238) and Paul Revere (240) both Columbia acts, 30 years apart. Same label, same region. Engine documents label pattern across eras.
theory_nodes.description: Columbia Records' two appearances in the 237-240 degree run.

theory_nodes.concept: Where the Action Is (1965-1967): Dick Clark's daily ABC TV show with Raiders as house band. Daily national television exposure before MTV. Revolutionary War costumes translate perfectly to visual medium. Costumes as marketing tool in television age.
theory_nodes.description: Television as the Raiders' primary commercial vehicle.

cross_references.relationship: Both early-1960s Pacific Northwest acts. Adjacent degrees. Surf Rock (E=5) to Garage Rock (E=6). Instrumental to vocal.
Columbia Records connection. Both Columbia. PUSA (1990s) and Raiders (1960s). Same label across 30 years.

sps_pair.pairing_note: TWO-THIRDS MARK. E=6 (Raiders) + E=2 (Bull Trout) = E=8. Most dramatic S36-SPS energy contrast in this four-degree run. Theatrical costumes + garage rock energy vs. the silent fish hiding in cold clear water. The band is loud; the Bull Trout makes almost no sound.

milestones: FIRST BOISE/IDAHO ORIGIN BAND in engine
